title: Network Segmentation Monitoring

dataSources: Industrial firewall logs, network flow data at IT/OT boundary
spl:
index=network sourcetype="pan:traffic" zone_pair="IT-to-OT"
| where action="allow"
| stats count by src, dest, dest_port, app
| sort -count
description: IT/OT network boundary violations create cybersecurity risk to critical infrastructure. Continuous monitoring validates segmentation.
value: IT/OT network boundary violations create cybersecurity risk to critical infrastructure. Continuous monitoring validates segmentation.
implementation: Forward IT/OT boundary firewall logs. Monitor all traffic crossing the boundary. Alert on unexpected protocols or connections. Validate against whitelist of approved communications. Report for ICS security audits.
visualization: Table (cross-boundary traffic), Sankey diagram (IT→OT flows), Bar chart (by protocol), Single value (unauthorized connections).
knownFalsePositives: Spikes during known cross-zone transfers, file drops, or vendor activities already approved in the change record.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Firewall TAs, network flow data.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Industrial firewall logs, network flow data at IT/OT boundary.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Forward IT/OT boundary firewall logs. Monitor all traffic crossing the boundary. Alert on unexpected protocols or connections. Validate against whitelist of approved communications. Report for ICS security audits.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=network sourcetype="pan:traffic" zone_pair="IT-to-OT"
| where action="allow"
| stats count by src, dest, dest_port, app
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Network Segmentation Monitoring** — IT/OT network boundary violations create cybersecurity risk to critical infrastructure. Continuous monitoring validates segmentation.

Documented **Data sources**: Industrial firewall logs, network flow data at IT/OT boundary. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Firewall TAs, network flow data. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: network; **sourcetype**: pan:traffic. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=network, sourcetype="pan:traffic". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where action="allow"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by src, dest, dest_port, app** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search when the process is in a known state. Compare key fields and alarm states to the SCADA or DCS HMI and, where relevant, the engineering tool for the PLC. Confirm sourcetypes and field extractions match the vendor feed.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (cross-boundary traffic), Sankey diagram (IT→OT flows), Bar chart (by protocol), Single value (unauthorized connections).
grandmaExplanation: We watch your plant control systems and field data. We help you know if something is off with this area: network segmentation before harm, damage, or wasted effort piles up.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable (no CIM or Operational_Telemetry data model in this SPL).
